title: API: images list response contains three additional fields not listed in reference
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

The images list API endpoint returns three additional fields which are not listed in the reference: `Names`, `Digest`, `History`

This affects version 3.4.x, I have not tested the development version 4.x since the project I'm working on is targeting 3.4.x and 4.x will contain breaking API changes.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. ``podman system service unix:///home/`whoami`/testing.sock --log-level=debug --time=500``

2. ``curl -X GET --unix-socket "/home/`whoami`/testing.sock" "http://d/v3.2.0/libpod/images/json"``


**Describe the results you received:**
```
[
   {
      "Id":"ed210e3e4a5bae1237f1bb44d72a05a2f1e5c6bfe7a7e73da179e2534269c459",
      "ParentId":"",
      "RepoTags":[
         "k8s.gcr.io/pause:3.5"
      ],
      "RepoDigests":[
         "sha256:1ff6c18fbef2045af6b9c16bf034cc421a29027b800e4f9b68ae9b1cb3e9ae07",
         "sha256:369201a612f7b2b585a8e6ca99f77a36bcdbd032463d815388a96800b63ef2c8"
      ],
      "Created":1615900617,
      "Size":689969,
      "SharedSize":0,
      "VirtualSize":689969,
      "Labels":null,
      "Containers":1,
      "Names":[
         "k8s.gcr.io/pause:3.5"
      ],
      "Digest":"sha256:1ff6c18fbef2045af6b9c16bf034cc421a29027b800e4f9b68ae9b1cb3e9ae07",
      "History":[
         "k8s.gcr.io/pause:3.5"
      ]
   },
]
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
```
[
   {
      "Containers":0,
      "Created":0,
      "Id":"string",
      "Labels":{
         "property1":"string",
         "property2":"string"
      },
      "ParentId":"string",
      "RepoDigests":[
         "string"
      ],
      "RepoTags":[
         "string"
      ],
      "SharedSize":0,
      "Size":0,
      "VirtualSize":0
   }
]
```


**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      3.4.2-dev
API Version:  3.4.2-dev
Go Version:   go1.15.9
Git Commit:   09aade7816e096550e805869f1300d7198aa8f91
Built:        Thu Oct 21 13:36:11 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```


**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md)**


Yes, latest version of 3.4.x built from source.

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
Windows 10, WSL, Debian Bullseye, 64bit

comments.body: Does the additional fields cause problems, IE Can your tool just ignore them?

Additional fields that are found in development / testing aren't a problem but lead to a very frustrating developer experience.
Unknown additional fields cause problems since the schema in the reference (or what is found in testing) is strictly enforced. Any difference in spelling or additional fields causes errors.

This is interesting: there is a name clash between ours and `docker`'s (vendor dir) `ImageSummary` struct. So even if we do changes to our struct generated swagger has origin in `docker`'s struct. 

I managed to force generator to also use our `ImageSummary`:
```go
// Image summary
// swagger:response DockerImageSummary
type swagImageSummary struct {
	// in:body
	Body []swagLibpodImageSummary
}

// swagger:model LibpodImageSummary
type swagLibpodImageSummary struct { entities.ImageSummary }
```
but there is a problem: it not only generates definition of `LibpodImageSummary` but it also somehow modifies original `docker`'s `ImageSummary` which is not nice.

```diff
2243a2244,2245
>       ConfigDigest:
>         type: string
2251a2254,2261
>       Dangling:
>         type: boolean
>       Digest:
>         type: string
>       History:
>         items:
>           type: string
>         type: array
2260a2271,2275
>       Names:
>         description: Podman extensions
>         items:
>           type: string
>         type: array
2264a2280,2281
>       ReadOnly:
>         type: boolean
2299c2316
<     x-go-package: github.com/containers/podman/vendor/github.com/docker/docker/api/types
---
>     x-go-package: github.com/containers/podman/pkg/domain/entities
3525a3543,3596
>   LibpodImageSummary:
>     properties:
>       ConfigDigest:
>         type: string
>       Containers:
>         format: int64
>         type: integer
>       Created:
>         format: int64
>         type: integer
>       Dangling:
>         type: boolean
>       Digest:
>         type: string
>       History:
>         items:
>           type: string
>         type: array
>       Id:
>         type: string
>         x-go-name: ID
>       Labels:
>         additionalProperties:
>           type: string
>         type: object
>       Names:
>         description: Podman extensions
>         items:
>           type: string
>         type: array
>       ParentId:
>         type: string
>       ReadOnly:
>         type: boolean
>       RepoDigests:
>         items:
>           type: string
>         type: array
>       RepoTags:
>         items:
>           type: string
>         type: array
>       SharedSize:
>         format: int64
>         type: integer
>       Size:
>         format: int64
>         type: integer
>       VirtualSize:
>         format: int64
>         type: integer
>     type: object
>     x-go-name: swagLibpodImageSummary
>     x-go-package: github.com/containers/podman/pkg/api/server
11016c11087
<           $ref: '#/responses/DocsLibpodImagesRemoveResponse'
---
>           $ref: '#/responses/DocsImageDeleteResponse'
11478c11549
<           $ref: '#/responses/DocsLibpodPruneResponse'
---
>           $ref: '#/responses/DocsImageDeleteResponse'
13337c13408
<         $ref: '#/definitions/ImageSummary'
---
>         $ref: '#/definitions/LibpodImageSummary'
```

So only proper solution would be rename `podman`'s `ImageSummary`. But that's API breaking so no go.
Or maybe another option would be not to rename `entities.ImageSummary`, but rather create identical copy of it in `swagger.go` with name `LibpodImageSummary`. But that's not DRY.

Another option would be not to include `docker`'s structs by using `-x` flag: `swagger generate spec -o ${SWAGGER_OUT} -i tags.yaml -w ./ -m -x github.com/containers/podman/vendor/github.com/docker/*` but I am not sure whether we want that.
OK I created a fix, still I suspect that very same issue may possibly affect some other types.
